humanSummary.text: Independent audit found no P0/P1 findings. CATALOG-002 satisfies the alpha blocking criteria; three P2 hardening findings were converted into follow-up DAG nodes.

findings.title: Completeness API schema accepts arbitrary enum strings
findings.description: The CATALOG-002 API response validator checks several enum-backed fields with generic string assertions instead of constraining them to the canonical enum values.

evidence.detail: Audit found status, confidence, rawContentRedactionClass, catalogSource, sourceRecordKind, conflict status, report pool, and report status accepted as arbitrary non-empty strings.

findings.impact: A malformed API response could pass schema validation and later be treated as a valid completeness status or provenance class.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Completeness API response schema validates status, confidence, redaction class, source kind, record kind, conflict status, pool, and report status against canonical enum constants.
Negative API schema fixtures reject at least one invalid value for each enum-backed CATALOG-002 response group.

proposedDagNode.title: Completeness API enum response guards
proposedDagNode.summary: Tighten CATALOG-002 API response validation so enum-backed completeness, provenance, conflict, and report fields reject arbitrary strings.
deliverables: Negative API schema fixtures for invalid completeness response enum values
acceptanceCriteria: Response validation rejects invalid language status, confidence, redaction class, source kind, source record kind, conflict status, report pool, and report status values.
Valid CATALOG-002 fixture responses continue to pass without weakening field presence checks.
auditFocus: Arbitrary strings accepted for enum-backed API fields
Validator constants drifting from DB/repository enums

findings.title: Public completeness reports can double-count overlapping pools
findings.description: The public completeness report increments aggregate conflict and status counts per pool occurrence, so a work that appears in overlapping pools can inflate aggregate counts.

evidence.detail: Audit identified that pool-local iteration can count the same work, conflict, or language status more than once in aggregate public report totals.

findings.impact: Public opportunity reports can overstate completeness evidence when benchmark pools overlap.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Public report aggregate counts are computed over unique work ids, conflict ids, and language status ids.
Pool-local work counts remain pool-local and intentionally allow overlap.
A DB-backed overlap fixture proves aggregate counts do not double-count a work present in multiple pools.

proposedDagNode.title: Completeness report overlap-safe aggregation
proposedDagNode.summary: Make public completeness report aggregate counts unique across overlapping benchmark and opportunity pools while preserving pool-local work counts.
deliverables: DB-backed overlap fixture for multi-pool works and conflicts
acceptanceCriteria: Aggregate status and conflict counts use unique language status and conflict identities.
A work appearing in fan_partial and conflict pools contributes once to aggregate work/status/conflict totals where applicable.
Pool-local work counts still describe each pool independently.
auditFocus: Aggregate counts inflated by overlapping pools
Pool-local and global report semantics collapsed

findings.title: Completeness conflict evidence subjects are not identity-checked
findings.description: Conflict evidence stores polymorphic subject kind and subject id as text, and repository validation currently checks only enum/non-empty shape rather than whether the subject belongs to the same normalized work.

evidence.detail: Audit found no rejection path for dangling or cross-work evidence subjects inside CATALOG-002 conflict evidence writes.

findings.impact: Conflict evidence can point to stale or unrelated records, weakening auditability of translation-completeness disputes.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Completeness conflict evidence subjects are validated against records for the same normalized work during writes, or stored in typed FK-backed tables/triggers.
Repository tests reject dangling subject ids and subject ids belonging to a different work.
Existing valid conflict evidence fixtures remain accepted.

proposedDagNode.title: Completeness conflict evidence identity validation
proposedDagNode.summary: Prevent CATALOG-002 conflict evidence from dangling or pointing across normalized works by validating evidence subject identity during writes.
acceptanceCriteria: Conflict evidence writes reject unknown subject ids for the declared subject kind.
Conflict evidence writes reject subject ids that belong to a different normalized work.
Valid same-work language status and source record evidence remains accepted.
